title: FOIA US Navy record  5/18 Vietnam War

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Military service records for subject below;
Name: Walter Gouveia
Branch: United States Navy
DOB: Sept 20, 1936
Dates of Service: Approx 1955 to 1980
passed away 2015

Request following documents;
1) copy of Bronze Star Medal award citations
2) copy of History of Assignments NAVPERS form 601-5
3) copy of Navy commendation medal award citation
4)  training history NAVPERS form 601-4
5) copy of Presidential Unit Citation award
5326   
Redact per 5 U.S.C. § 552(b)(6).
I request hard copies of paperwork in the file showing this information.
Please mail hardcopies to:
MUCKROCK NEWS
263 HUNTINGTON AVE
BOSTON, MA 02115
or please email pdf copies to requests@muckrock.com

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley

title: National Climate Assessment (National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request all communications between this agency and the White House, Executive Office of the President, Office of Management and Budget, Office of Science and Technology Policy, Council on Environmental Quality, or Department of Government Efficiency concerning the National Climate Assessment, globalchange.gov, USGCRP, climate webpages, climate datasets, climate reports, or public access to climate science materials.

Please include emails, calendar invitations, attachments, memoranda, Teams/Slack messages, briefing materials, and meeting notes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Lauren Pearle

title: Untitled (Office of Science and Technology Policy)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request records concerning legal review of the removal, disabling, discontinuation, archiving, or reduced public availability of National Climate Assessment materials, globalchange.gov, or other U.S. Global Change Research Program materials.

Please include legal memoranda, attorney-client communications where segregable factual material can be released, records discussing the Global Change Research Act of 1990, records discussing statutory obligations to produce or disseminate the National Climate Assessment, and records discussing litigation risk, records retention, the Federal Records Act, FOIA, public access, or scientific integrity obligations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Lauren Pearle

title: National Climate Assessment (National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request records showing changes to federal webpages or public-facing digital assets related to the National Climate Assessment and globalchange.gov from June 1, 2025, through July 15, 2025.

This includes content-management-system logs, GitHub or code repository commits, web publishing logs, redirect records, deletion logs, access-control changes, DNS records, web server configuration changes, help-desk tickets, Jira tickets, ServiceNow tickets, contractor work orders, and records identifying the user, office, contractor, or system account that made or approved the changes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Lauren Pearle

title: Removal of National Climate Assessment materials (Office of Science and Technology Policy)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request all records identifying, discussing, approving, transmitting, or implementing any instruction, recommendation, order, directive, request, or guidance to remove, disable, redirect, archive, decommission, or otherwise limit public access to National Climate Assessment materials or the globalchange.gov website.

This request includes records showing the names, titles, offices, agencies, contractors, or White House components involved in making, approving, transmitting, or implementing the decision.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Lauren Pearle

title: Removal of National Climate Assessments (U.s. Global Change Research Program)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I would like to request records from Jan. 20, 2025, to the present concerning the removal, disabling, redirection, archiving, migration, or loss of public access to federal webpages, PDFs, portals, datasets, or other online materials related to the National Climate Assessment, including but not limited to the First, Second, Third, Fourth and Fifth National Climate Assessments and any webpages formerly available through globalchange.gov.

Please include emails, memoranda, decision documents, meeting agendas, meeting notes, talking points, directives, web tickets, content-management-system records, contracts, task orders, legal reviews, and communications with contractors.

Search terms should include: “National Climate Assessment,” “NCA,” “NCA5,” “Fifth National Climate Assessment,” “globalchange.gov,” “USGCRP,” “remove,” “removed,” “offline,” “redirect,” “archive,” “decommission,” “web content,” “website,” “public access,” “climate assessment,” and “take down.”

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Lauren Pearle

title: FOIA US Army record 5/16

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA US Army record 5/16
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Military service records concerning below:
Name: Phillip D. Clarkson
Branch: United States Army
DOB: Feb 17, 1943
Dates of Service: 1962 to 1985
passed away 2018

Please provide the following documents:
1. copy of Bronze Star Medal award citation
2. copy of Army commendation award citations 
3. Section VII CURRENT AND PREVIOUS ASSIGNMENTS form
4. Section II Military Education/ training courses form


I request hard copies of paperwork in the file showing this information. Redact per U.S.C. § 552(b)(6).
Please mail hardcopies to:
MUCKROCK NEWS
263 HUNTINGTON AVE
BOSTON, MA 02115

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley

Name: David Rutherford
Branch: United States Navy
service number: B208215
Dates of Service: about 1965 to 1974

Request following documents;
1) copy of Bronze Star Medal award citation
2) copy of Occupational training history NAVPERS form 601-4

Redact per 5 U.S.C. § 552(b)(6).

Please mail hardcopies to:

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley
